message:
chore(beads): sync issues.jsonl after PolicyDecision + HealthState pin landings

Small follow-up sync (~2 lines drift) absorbing updated_at / close_reason
field updates produced when the parallel agents marked the
flywheel_connectors-qyq9l ("PolicyDecision serde tag matrix") and
flywheel_connectors-e47ej ("HealthState transitions") beads complete.
Equivalent to running `bd sync` immediately after the two test commits in
this batch.

No source code, test code, or configuration changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -11,7 +11,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0cncv","title":"[HARDENING][MEDIUM] fcp-bootstrap cert validity lacks clock-skew tolerance","description":"crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:1223 and :1249 both call parsed.validity().is_valid() to check X.509 NotBefore/NotAfter against system time, with no skew tolerance. A machine with clock drift (cold-boot before NTP sync, VM clock, etc.) can silently reject freshly-provisioned certificates whose NotBefore is seconds in the future from the host's perspective, producing a cryptic NoVerifiedIssuerChain error with no hint that it's a clock issue.

Standard PKI practice is to accept certs within a small skew window (±60s typical).

Fix: add a helper cert_valid_with_skew(&parsed, 60_secs) that uses parsed.validity().not_before / .not_after and OffsetDateTime::now_utc() directly, accepting a forward-skew on NotBefore. Keep NotAfter strict (never accept expired). Apply at both call sites.

Not a vulnerability — defensive hardening for cold-bootstrap robustness on clock-skewed machines.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T03:58:52.658144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T04:27:38.370550Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T04:27:38.370246Z","close_reason":"Completed: added 60s NotBefore bootstrap skew tolerance while keeping NotAfter strict, with certificate-selection regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0cwuw","title":"TEST-FUZZ-RATELIMIT-CONFIG: fuzz rate limit config parser","description":"Add a cargo-fuzz target for fcp_core::RateLimit JSON parsing and validate() so arbitrary rate-limit objects cannot desynchronize zero-bound checks, scope parsing, pool-name grammar, and parsed_scope defaults.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod5","created_at":"2026-04-28T00:32:03.188117Z","created_by":"cod5","updated_at":"2026-04-28T00:34:54.385319Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T00:34:54.385077Z","close_reason":"Completed: shipped RateLimit parser/validator fuzz target with rch build proof","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","fuzz","tests"],"comments":[{"id":3189,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0cwuw","author":"cod5","text":"cod5 shipped fuzz_rate_limit_config_parse. The target drives fcp_core::RateLimit with structured arbitrary values plus arbitrary bounded JSON, then checks validate() error precedence, scope parsing/defaults, pool-name grammar, serde round-trip preservation, and parsed_scope() consistency. Verification: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod5-fixed cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin fuzz_rate_limit_config_parse passed; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod5-fixed cargo fmt --check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml passed; cargo fuzz list includes fuzz_rate_limit_config_parse. Note: the check also surfaced a pre-existing fcp-hackernews deprecated CapabilityVerifier::verify warning from the already-wired Hacker News dependency, unrelated to this target.","created_at":"2026-04-28T00:34:41Z"}]}

Replace it with real preflight denial behavior and regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:59:39.738765Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T21:06:16.844096Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T21:06:16.843642Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["arxiv","fix","mock-code"],"comments":[{"id":3024,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0fo8l","author":"jemanuel","text":"51eaf433 implements arxiv simulate preflight hardening: supported operations now deny when connector readiness or required inputs fail instead of returning placeholder allowed=true. Verification: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod2-fixed cargo check -p fcp-arxiv --all-targets; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod2-fixed cargo test --manifest-path connectors/arxiv/Cargo.toml --lib simulate -- --nocapture; ubs --only=rust --staged (0 critical).","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:06:11Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0hbjk","title":"FIX-ZALO: use valid manifest archetypes","description":"mock-code-finder pass on remaining O-Z surfaces found the Zalo connector manifest is still scaffold-shaped enough that canonical fwc manifest parsing fails: connector.archetypes includes polling and webhook, but fcp-manifest currently accepts only bidirectional, streaming, operational, storage, and knowledge. This blocks manifest hash validation and makes the advertised surface untrustworthy. Replace invalid archetypes with valid conservative values and sync the interface hash; verify with connector-local cargo and fwc manifest check.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T00:03:43.814829Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T00:05:27.177446Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T00:05:27.176975Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","security"],"comments":[{"id":3171,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0hbjk","author":"jemanuel","text":"e8a55758 implements Zalo manifest truthfulness hardening from mock-code-finder: replaces invalid archetypes polling/webhook with accepted bidirectional/streaming/operational values, removes stale connector.status, and syncs the interface hash. Verified without rch: fwc manifest fix connectors/zalo/manifest.toml --check --json; cargo fmt --check --manifest-path Cargo.toml from connectors/zalo; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod4-fixed cargo test --tests from connectors/zalo. UBS scanned connectors/zalo; fmt/clippy/check/test-build phases were clean, but exit was nonzero from existing test panic/unwrap inventories.","created_at":"2026-04-28T00:05:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0iek1","title":"[fcp-e2e] Enable kill_on_drop for connector subprocess runner","description":"crates/fcp-e2e/src/subprocess.rs claimed to avoid zombie leaks in Drop, but it only called Child::start_kill(). The underlying asupersync Child docs state start_kill/kill do not wait and still require wait() to reap. Switch the spawned command to kill_on_drop(true), remove the misleading manual Drop path, and add regression coverage that a dropped long-running runner no longer leaves its PID present.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:44:41.568206Z","created_by":"ChartreuseCardinal","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:32:33.422700Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T19:32:33.422177Z","close_reason":"Stale-complete: kill_on_drop implementation and regression coverage already present in worktree","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1993,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0iek1","author":"VioletSummit","text":"Verified current worktree state for this bead.

Findings:
- `crates/fcp-e2e/src/subprocess.rs` already sets `Command::kill_on_drop(true)` in `ConnectorProcessRunner::spawn()`.
- The old manual-Drop approach is no longer present.
- A Unix regression test exists at `drop_reaps_long_running_process`, which checks that dropping a long-running runner makes its PID disappear.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-e2e --all-targets` passed.
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt -p fcp-e2e -- --check` passed.
- `ubs crates/fcp-e2e/src/subprocess.rs` is not trustworthy for this single Rust file in this repo state; UBS misclassified the path across multiple language scanners and emitted obvious false positives like JavaScript loose-equality warnings on Rust syntax.
- A targeted `rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-e2e --lib drop_reaps_long_running_process -- --nocapture` did not reach the `fcp-e2e` test body because the remote build hit an unrelated compile blocker in `crates/fcp-conformance/src/vectors/hpke.rs` on the worker, despite the local worktree already containing a fix for that line.
- Earlier remote test runs also hit separate `rch` artifact-retrieval failures caused by pathological nested paths under `.beads/recovery_*`; that is operational fallout, not evidence against this bead implementation.

Conclusion: the requested `kill_on_drop` change and regression coverage are already present in the current worktree, so this bead is stale-complete from a backlog perspective. Remaining noise is in unrelated verification infrastructure.","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:32:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0j4qx","title":"Pin fcp-core ZoneId equality across construction paths","description":"Add tests in crates/fcp-core/tests/ pinning ZoneId equality: same-zone constructed via FromStr vs Display vs literal, and inequality across distinct zones.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"cc1","created_at":"2026-04-28T19:54:39.471138Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:02:52.077193Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T20:02:52.076943Z","close_reason":"Shipped: crates/fcp-core/tests/zone_id_equality_across_paths.rs (14/14) pins ZoneId equality + hash across every construction path (factory / try_from / FromStr / parse / Display→try_from / clone / Tailscale tag round-trip), inequality across distinct canonical zones + project subzones (z:work ≠ z:project:work), Tailscale tag format pinned per zone, HashMap-key correctness via every path, as_str/Display/as_bytes view agreement, and validation rejection (empty / non-z-prefix / uppercase).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-pmjio","title":"TEST-CONFORMANCE-49: fcp-host resilience retry/circuit-breaker/bulkhead routing-decision conformance","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:58:32.725483Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T00:00:28.123955Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T00:00:28.123488Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3166,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pmjio","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped 39 conformance tests in crates/fcp-conformance/tests/host_resilience_routing_decision_conformance.rs. The ACTUAL host-side retry-and-routing primitive — ResilienceLayer (circuit breaker + bulkhead + health router + load shedder). Zero prior coverage. Pinned every documented default: circuit (3 fail / 2 success / 5s open / 30s window / AnyError), bulkhead (16 concurrent / 32 queued / 250ms timeout), health (3 unhealthy / 2 recovery / 750ms latency / 500‰ error rate / 5s probe / 30s window / 200‰ EWMA alpha), load shed (850‰ start / 1000‰ full / [Low, Normal] sheddable — Critical+High NEVER shed). RoutingDecision 4 variants, CircuitState 3 variants (Closed start), RequestPriority 4-distinct + Hash, FailurePredicate 4-distinct. ResilienceError Display for all 8 variants pinned (incident log greps). ResilienceMetricsSnapshot default all-zero. 39/39 green in 0.00s. Commit 0ebefdeb.","created_at":"2026-04-28T00:00:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-pmosg","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-webhook stripe_provider_vector compliance test broken on main (hard-coded stale timestamp)","description":"crates/fcp-webhook/tests/webhook_receiver_compliance.rs:129-175 (commit 08dcdf06 'cap Slack timestamp header size') — the new stripe_provider_vector_known_good_and_tampered_payloads compliance test uses a static timestamp 1_700_000_000 (Nov 14, 2023) and tolerance 5s, but verify_and_parse calls Utc::now(). Actual failure today: TimestampValidation { reason: 'Timestamp outside tolerance window', timestamp: Some(1700000000), current_time: 1776967230, tolerance: 5s }. Test has been broken since landing — the fcp-webhook crate's cargo test will fail on main. Repro: 
running 1 test
test stripe_provider_vector_known_good_and_tampered_payloads ... FAILED

failures:

---- stripe_provider_vector_known_good_and_tampered_payloads stdout ----

thread 'stripe_provider_vector_known_good_and_tampered_payloads' (943841) panicked at crates/fcp-webhook/tests/webhook_receiver_compliance.rs:152:18:
known-good Stripe vector must verify: TimestampValidation { reason: "Timestamp outside tolerance window", timestamp: Some(1700000000), current_time: 1776967241, tolerance: 5s }
note: run with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1` environment variable to display a backtrace
{"assertions":{"failed":5,"passed":0},"correlation_id":"fcp-webhook::stripe_provider_vector_known_good_and_tampered_payloads","details":null,"duration_us":144,"level":"info","module":"fcp-webhook","operation":"webhook.stripe.vector","phase":"verify","result":"fail","test_name":"stripe_provider_vector_known_good_and_tampered_payloads","timestamp":"2026-04-23T18:00:41.373Z"}


failures:
    stripe_provider_vector_known_good_and_tampered_payloads

test result: FAILED. 0 passed; 1 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 5 filtered out; finished in 0.00s -> FAILED; 0 passed 1 failed. Suggested fix: use StripeWebhook::with_fixed_now_for_tests (added in commit 03665486) OR expose it with a public builder + call it here OR compute the timestamp dynamically via Utc::now().timestamp() and regenerate the signature at test time. Reviewer: cc_1.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:00:41.481260Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:10:51.395165Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:10:51.394754Z","close_reason":"Generate timestamp + signature dynamically at test time so verify_and_parse (which reads Utc::now()) accepts the vector. The structural/HMAC/tamper contract of the compliance test is preserved; bytes are no longer pinned but handler has no public test-clock override to enable true byte-fixed vectors. Verified: 6/6 tests pass in webhook_receiver_compliance binary.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-poz0p","title":"Pin fcp-core CheckOutcome predicate matrix","description":"Add tests in crates/fcp-core/tests/ pinning CheckOutcome::is_allow/is_deny/is_skip predicates per documented contract.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"cod4","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:04:46.779188Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:05:27.063647Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-poz0p","title":"Pin fcp-core CheckOutcome predicate matrix","description":"Add tests in crates/fcp-core/tests/ pinning CheckOutcome::is_allow/is_deny/is_skip predicates per documented contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"cod4","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:04:46.779188Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:31:24.032922Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T20:31:24.032664Z","close_reason":"Completed in 53a7d5e8c; verified with rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-core","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-pp2s6","title":"[REVIEW R2 P4][MED] fcp-e2e/amplitude: adapter invents accepted handshake after session-only call","description":"crates/fcp-e2e/tests/amplitude_compliance_e2e.rs:65-95 calls handle_handshake(json!({"session_id": "amplitude-e2e-session"})) instead of sending the real HandshakeRequest, then fabricates an accepted HandshakeResponse with SessionId::new(), a hardcoded manifest_hash, and capabilities_granted copied from req.capabilities_requested. This bypasses the connector handshake contract entirely. Fix: pass the real request into handle_handshake, parse the connector response, and assert the returned handshake fields rather than synthesizing them locally.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:51:20.700387Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T20:10:36.923620Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T20:10:36.923126Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2848,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-pp2s6","author":"jemanuel","text":"Resolved in commit 227a4415. Same pattern as spotify/segment/llm-router etc.: adapter now (1) generates SessionId::new, (2) serializes the real HandshakeRequest (named FcpError::Internal on failure), (3) injects session_id into the params bag, (4) calls handle_handshake and captures the response, (5) asserts the ad-hoc envelope contract (protocol_version=='2.0', connector_id present, capabilities array present), (6) intersects capabilities_requested with the connector's advertised capability list so the connector cannot widen the grant. Verified: cargo test -p fcp-e2e --test amplitude_compliance_e2e — 3/3 pass. — cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-24T20:10:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ptr","title":"fcp.anthropic - Anthropic AI Provider Connector","description":"# FCP Anthropic Connector - Anthropic AI Provider (LEGACY / SUPERSEDED)

> NOTE (2026-01-15): This bead predates the current **FCP2 connector model** and is kept only for historical context of an earlier prototype.
> 
> The active, FCP2-compliant Anthropic plan is: `flywheel_connectors-lszk.4` (and its child beads). Use that as the source of truth.

## Overview
A comprehensive connector for Anthropic's Claude API, enabling agents to leverage Claude models for complex reasoning, tool use, vision capabilities, and batch processing with built-in usage tracking and budget enforcement.

## Priority: P1 (Critical Infrastructure)

## Core Capabilities

### 1. Messages API
- Send messages to Claude models
- Streaming responses for real-time output
- Multi-turn conversations
- System prompts
- Model selection (claude-opus-4-5-20251101, claude-sonnet-4-20250514, claude-haiku, etc.)
- Temperature and sampling controls
- Max tokens configuration
- Stop sequences

### 2. Tool Use Support
- Define tools with JSON schema
- Handle tool_use responses
- Execute tools and return results
- Multi-tool conversations
- Parallel tool execution
- Tool choice forcing (auto, any, specific)

### 3. Vision Capabilities
- Images in prompts (base64 or URL)
- Multiple images per message
- Image analysis and description
- Document/screenshot understanding
- Chart and diagram interpretation

### 4. Prompt Caching
- Cache system prompts
- Cache large context documents
- Cache tool definitions
- Automatic cache management
- Cache hit/miss tracking
- Usage/cost savings can be computed from tracked usage deltas

### 5. Batch API
- Submit batch requests
- Track batch status
- Retrieve batch results
- Cancel batches
- Batch pricing can change; treat billing as provider- or policy-owned (do not hardcode discounts)

### 6. Usage Tracking & Budget Enforcement
- Per-request usage calculation (tokens, images, audio seconds, etc.)
- Running total tracking
- Budget limits per agent/session/day
- Alerts at thresholds
- Automatic cutoff at budget limit
- Usage attribution and reporting

## FCP Integration

### Manifest Structure
```toml
[connector]
name = "fcp.anthropic"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Anthropic Claude API integration with usage tracking"

[capabilities]
messages = { risk = "medium", description = "Send messages to Claude" }
streaming = { risk = "medium", description = "Stream responses" }
tool_use = { risk = "medium", description = "Use tools in conversations" }
vision = { risk = "medium", description = "Process images" }
batch = { risk = "low", description = "Submit batch requests" }

[trust_zones]
default = "z:ai:inference"

[resources]
rate_limit_rpm = 1000
rate_limit_tpm = 100000
default_timeout_ms = 120000

[billing]
track_costs = true
budget_enforcement = true
```

### Tool Definitions
```yaml
tools:
  - name: anthropic_message
    description: Send a message to Claude
    parameters:
      model:
        type: string
        enum: [claude-opus-4-5-20251101, claude-sonnet-4-20250514, claude-3-5-haiku-20241022]
        required: true
      messages:
        type: array
        items:
          type: object
          properties:
            role: { type: string, enum: [user, assistant] }
            content: { type: string }
        required: true
      system: { type: string }
      max_tokens: { type: integer, default: 4096 }
      temperature: { type: number, default: 1.0, min: 0, max: 1 }
      stream: { type: boolean, default: false }

  - name: anthropic_message_with_tools
    description: Send a message with tool definitions
    parameters:
      model: { type: string, required: true }
      messages: { type: array, required: true }
      tools:
        type: array
        items:
          type: object
          properties:
            name: { type: string }
            description: { type: string }
            input_schema: { type: object }
      tool_choice:
        type: object
        properties:
          type: { type: string, enum: [auto, any, tool] }
          name: { type: string }

  - name: anthropic_message_with_vision
    description: Send a message with images
    parameters:
      model: { type: string, required: true }
      messages:
        type: array
        items:
          type: object
          properties:
            role: { type: string }
            content:
              type: array
              items:
                oneOf:
                  - type: object
                    properties:
                      type: { const: text }
                      text: { type: string }
                  - type: object
                    properties:
                      type: { const: image }
                      source:
                        type: object
                        properties:
                          type: { enum: [base64, url] }
                          media_type: { type: string }
                          data: { type: string }

  - name: anthropic_batch_create
    description: Create a batch request
    parameters:
      requests:
        type: array
        items:
          type: object
          properties:
            custom_id: { type: string }
            params: { type: object }
        required: true

  - name: anthropic_batch_status
    description: Get batch status
    parameters:
      batch_id: { type: string, required: true }

  - name: anthropic_batch_results
    description: Get batch results
    parameters:
      batch_id: { type: string, required: true }

  - name: anthropic_get_usage
    description: Get current usage metrics (currency cost is policy/ops-owned)
    parameters:
      period: { type: string, enum: [session, day, month] }
```

## Usage / Cost Tracking (NO hard-coded pricing)

### Why we do NOT embed pricing tables
Provider pricing changes frequently. Hard-coding a “pricing model” table in a connector/spec becomes incorrect quickly.

### What we track instead (stable)
- provider-reported usage fields (when present)
- token counts (input/output/cache)
- image counts/size classes
- audio seconds

### Budget enforcement
Budgets can be enforced in two stable ways:
1. **Usage budgets** (preferred): token/image/second limits per request/session/day.
2. **Currency budgets** (optional): computed by an external, versioned “pricing catalog” object owned by policy/ops (not embedded in the connector).

### Attribution
- Track usage per agent
- Track usage per task/session
- Track usage per operation/tool
- Generate reports

## Prompt Caching Strategy
- System prompts > 1024 tokens: Always cache
- Tool definitions: Cache when > 5 tools
- Large documents: Cache with TTL
- Cache warming on connector init

## Error Handling
- Rate limit (429): Exponential backoff
- Overloaded (529): Queue and retry
- Invalid request (400): Parse and report
- Auth failure (401): Refresh credentials
- Context length exceeded: Truncation strategy

## Dependencies
- anthropic-rs or custom HTTP client
- reqwest (HTTP)
- tokio (async)
- serde (serialization)
- async-stream (streaming)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Send basic message and receive response
- [ ] Stream response tokens in real-time
- [ ] Use tools in conversation
- [ ] Process images in prompts
- [ ] Submit and retrieve batch jobs
- [ ] Track usage accurately per request
- [ ] Enforce budget limits
- [ ] Handle rate limits gracefully
- [ ] Utilize prompt caching

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-pvjd","title":"TEST: Automation Recipes Unit/Integration Tests (OAuth, Webhooks, Provisioning)","description":"# TEST: Automation Recipes Unit/Integration Tests

## Goal
Verify that connectors can be provisioned automatically without manual copy-paste steps.

## Test Categories

### 1. Provisioning Interface Tests
- Recipe object schema validation
- Required setup steps declaration
- Host-mediated setup flow execution
- Approval flow for privileged setup

### 2. OAuth Flow Tests (Mock Server)
- Redirect URL registration
- Authorization code exchange
- Token refresh flow
- Token revocation handling
- Secrets never touch disk
- Zeroize on completion

### 3. Webhook Flow Tests (Mock Server)
- Endpoint verification (challenge-response)
- Webhook registration
- Event routing to connector
- Webhook secret rotation

### 4. Polling Schedule Tests
- Cursor initialization
- Schedule configuration
- Backoff on rate limits
- Cursor persistence as mesh objects

### 5. Audit Integration Tests
- Privileged setup produces audit events
- Audit events contain correct evidence
- Approval flow audit trail

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON with recipe_id, step_name
- Secrets NEVER logged (verify with grep)
- Timing for each setup step
- Error details with recovery paths

## E2E Tests
- Full OAuth flow with mock provider
- Full webhook registration with mock server
- Recipe configures connector without manual steps

## Acceptance Criteria
- Mock servers are deterministic
- No real external API calls
- Secrets handled in memory only
- Audit trail is complete","notes":"2026-03-10 IndigoHare: expanded automation-recipe coverage in provisioning, OAuth, and webhook paths. Added structured provisioning step-log assertions (timing, prompt type, recovery hints, no secret leakage), a full PKCE auth-code->refresh mock-server lifecycle test, and a combined webhook registration/challenge/routing/secret-rotation lifecycle test.
